rulings.Case Title: HENRY GORING VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Svi Corporation For Reconsideration Of Its Motion For Summary Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 17, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant SVI Corp.'s motion for reconsideration of its motion for summary judgment is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. sections 1005(b) and 1010.6 and plaintiff objected on such grounds. Plaintiff's objection based on insufficient notice is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ANDRE WRIGHT VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 17, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Intervenors on behalf of their suspended insured Scott Co. of California's motion for summary judgment is denied and their alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 3 (negligence and premises owner/contractor liability), granted as to issue 2 (strict liability) and off calendar as to issue 4 (punitive damages). As to summary judgment and adjudication issues 1 and 3, intervenors sustained their initial burden. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Wright was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (gaskets) attributable to Scott Co. Intervenors' objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled, except for intervenors' objections to plaintiff's identification of Garlock-brand gaskets, which are sustained because plaintiff testified during his deposition that he did not know the brand or manufacturer of the gaskets. As to adjudication issue 2, intervenors sustained their initial burden and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether intervenors are liable under a strict liability theory. Plaintiff has dismissed his claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: WOODROW THOMAS VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Apply Texas Law
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 17, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s motion to apply Texas law: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45am. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)
